Scenario.title: Operation Ghost: Multi-Stage Cyber Espionage Against Communications Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Complex [A sophisticated multi-stage attack leveraging AI-driven reconnaissance, phishing, and lateral movement to disrupt critical communications networks]
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), Network Traffic, Internet-facing assets, Command, Windows Registry, Domain Name Registrar, OS Process, Process, File, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism), Delivery Method, Command Execution (Mechanism), Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism), Pass the Hash, Process Injection, Process Creation (Mechanism), Reg.exe Utility, Remote Desktop Protocol, File Modification (Mechanism), Process Creation (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers deploy AI algorithms to analyze public records, social media, and technical documentation, mapping the communications infrastructure's architecture and identifying vulnerable endpoints.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Malicious payloads are crafted using insights from network traffic analysis, enabling the attackers to develop spear-phishing campaigns tailored to specific organizational roles within the communications sector.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Deceptive emails containing malicious attachments are sent to employees, exploiting human error to deliver malware that compromises internet-facing systems and establishes an initial foothold.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-663: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: Malware is executed via document macros, leveraging command-line interfaces to exploit transient instruction execution flaws in processors to escalate privileges undetected.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A persistent backdoor is created in the Windows Registry to maintain access, mimicking legitimate credential prompts to avoid triggering security alerts.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used with pass-the-hash techniques to elevate privileges across domain-registered systems, granting unrestricted access to internal networks.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate processes to evade detection, masking its presence within normal system operations.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Compromised deployment tools are used to harvest credentials from memory during process creation, exfiltrating sensitive authentication data.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network sniffing utilities like Reg.exe are employed to map active devices and services, identifying high-value targets within the communications network.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Lateral movement occurs via compromised RDP sessions, allowing attackers to pivot across domain-registered systems and expand their control.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Sensitive communication data is collected by corrupting files and altering metadata to ensure exfiltration while leaving minimal traces of tampering.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is exfiltrated through process creation tunnels, disguising malicious traffic as legitimate network protocols to avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: Critical communications data is obfuscated within network traffic patterns, causing disruptions and operational chaos while evading forensic analysis.

Scenario.summary: [Operation Ghost demonstrates a coordinated attack chain combining AI reconnaissance, phishing, and advanced persistence techniques to infiltrate and disrupt communications infrastructure, highlighting the need for layered defense strategies against evolving cyber threats.]
